response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished senior full-stack software engineer with nearly two decades of experience, but his background is centered on web, mobile, and product development (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, React) rather than hardware performance architecture. The role requires SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture knowledge, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear in his experience. His closest relevant work (low-latency DLang microservices, C/C++ at National Instruments) demonstrates systems aptitude but falls far short of the principal-level hardware and deep-learning workload expertise this position demands.
strengths: 18+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, embedded, and systems domains
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (DLang microservices handling millions of requests per second with async I/O and fibers)
Some C/C++ systems-level work (NI LabWindows/CVI components, wave field synthesis audio thesis)
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware architecture, or hardware-software co-design experience
No AI/deep-learning workload, GPU architecture, or accelerator optimization background
Career trajectory is web/full-stack product engineering, not performance architecture at principal level

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware architecture experience
No AI accelerator or deep-learning workload background
